name: biomedical-agent-teams
description: Codex-native biomedical agent-team router for life-science research, hypothesis validation, literature and public-omics evidence synthesis, claim-level verification, statistical audit, experiment design, translational scouting, and scientific writing support.

interface.shortDescription: Protocol-locked biomedical research council with inline-first hybrid execution, selective spawned review, team-level DAGs, evidence, omics, audit, design, passport, and integrity gates.
interface.longDescription: A Codex-native biomedical agent-team suite for structured life-science research. It locks context of use, requires command-level preflight contracts, normalizes biomedical entities, coordinates specialist lanes for hypothesis discovery, literature retrieval, public omics analysis, mechanism and causal reasoning, experiment design, clinical/regulatory/IP scouting, and routes all claims through a fixed-field central claim ledger, contract-gated role outputs, biomedical passport state, audit gates, citation verification, ledger-only writing, excluded-claim tracking, integrity-gate failure-mode checks, inline-first hybrid execution, selective spawned reviewer lanes, dependency-aware team-level spawned workflows, and post-write validation.
defaultPrompt: biomedical-research-council TET2 KO plus IL-21 armored CAR-T persistence hypothesis
omics-analysis-team find public datasets to test IL-21-STAT3-FOXO1 stem-like memory signatures
omics-analysis-team TCGA survival analysis for DUSP5 high versus low in CD3-high solid tumors --track survival --mode plan
experiment-design-team validate whether IL-21-STAT3-FOXO1 supports stem-like CAR-T persistence --mode deep
evidence-audit-team verify this CAR-T mechanism claim with primary literature and public databases
translational-scout-team benchmark IL-21 armored CAR-T safety and IP differentiation --mode standard
